ads.author: Peyton Stroup
ads.title: Williams Pit Stop Pinball Machine
ads.description: Pinball machine from 1968. Works and plays. Picked this up a few years back for my fiance - has received a lot of play and love, but we're conveniently moving and thought we would give it a post to see if there's interest before we broke it down and packed it up. In a moment of genius, the last time I adjusted the legs, I put one back and front leg on the back AND front, if that makes sense, so it plays on a bit of a right lean. Can be fixed by just swapping the legs around the right way. The P1 score reel also sticks now and then, but comes back to shape after a few extra ticks. You can force it with a little contact switch in the back, but it always kicks up for us after a few seconds. You can see a game starting and a ball reloading in the video. Will not respond to "is this still available," first to come by is the first to take it.

ads.author: Dave Owens
ads.title: Swimways Coop SCATTER DODGEBALL Fun Dodgeball Game Set # 34701 - New
ads.description: A Scatter Dodgeball game set (Swimways/Coop, # 34701). The game set is new and still contained in the original packaging. The packaging does show some (see photos), but it doesn't affect the game components. A great game to add to your collection or to just play and enjoy. About the game... Each player begins with two same-colored wristbands. And the beginning of each round, players form a circle around the dodgeball and one player rolls the dice. Any player who is wearing a wristband that matches the color on the side of the dice when the dice comes to rest may pick up the dodgeball while the other players scatter. If player throwing the dodgeball throws and hits another player, that player has to give the thrower one of their wristbands. If player throws the dodgeball and another player catches it, then player throwing has to give one wristband to the player who caught the ball. First player to collect all the wristbands wins. Game includes: One sting-proof dodgeball. One colored foam dice. Twelve colored wristbands (6 colors). Game rules.

Beautifully illuminated with strong audio, animated reel action, and classic Japanese arcade/casino aesthetics. Machine features: * Working reel system * Illuminated bonus display * Skill-stop gameplay * Original internal electronics * Hopper/token system * Integrated speakers and sound * Japanese artwork and Okinawa-themed cabinet * Factory Takasago hardware and boards * Home-use converted setup Condition is exceptionally clean for its age and presents beautifully when powered on. ads.author: Olivia Treece
ads.title: Unknown Vintage 80s converted Atari Cabinet
ads.description: Open to offers! Available during Yard Sale Fri-Sat, May 8-9. 2 Ellzey Lane, Greenbrier. Just outside of Conway, next to Angel’s landing flea market. Atari arcade cabinet shell that appears to have been converted into a gambling/poker-style machine sometime in the late 80s. Cabinet still has original Atari tags/stickers on the back and retains the original CRT monitor. Machine powers on and is fully playable using the coin slot and keypad controls. Boots to a blue instruction screen for a gambling-style game. Coin mechanism is functional. Original CRT monitor works and still has the classic loud startup/degauss sound. Cabinet has been painted black at some point and is missing some original control panel pieces, but overall it’s a really interesting vintage arcade conversion piece. Features: Original Atari cabinet shell Working CRT monitor Functional coin mechanism Playable gambling/poker-style game Original Atari manufacturer plates/stickers still attached Vintage late 80s / early 90s conversion hardware Heavy, bring help to load. Pickup only. Available during Yard Sale Fri-Sat, May 8-9. 2 Ellzey Lane, Greenbrier. Just outside of Conway, next to Angel’s landing flea market.

ads.author: Nate Lembcke
ads.title: Gottlieb 300 Pinball Machine 1975
ads.description: 1975 Gottlieb 300 pinball machine with a bowling theme. It’s an EM (electro mechanical) 4 player game. Located in Springfield , GA 31329 I’ve had this for 9 months and it’s been a fun project and fun game play. Time to let it go, so I have room for the next project. All 4 players work. I have cleaned all the score reels. I removed almost everything from the playfield and cleaned it, touched up paint, leveled all the inserts, cleaned all the plastics, all new rubbers and waxed. All the posts have been upgraded to 3D printed bowling pins to match the theme. I have all the original ones if you want to swap back. It still takes quarters and has the original schematic and manual. The cabinet is yellowed like a lot of old EMs. The legs and coin door are heavily covered with surface rust. It’s missing the original back box metal door. The back glass has typical flaking and missing paint. All the bulbs are good, but some are flaky do to old bayonet style sockets (I didn’t want to put that much work into them to be honest).

Gameplay: Players control giant monsters (George, Lizzie, and Ralph) and destroy cities while battling military forces. Manual Type: This is an original operation manual with schematics for the arcade game. Significance: Rampage is a classic arcade game known for its unique monster-themed gameplay and has spawned numerous sequels and adaptations, including a 2018 film. For more information please call or message me. Located in Spencer Tennessee. [hidden information]

ads.author: Scott Nielsen
ads.title: Vintage Operation, Maintenance and Service Manual" for the classic arcade game, Kangaroo
ads.description: Vintage Operation, Maintenance and Service Manual" for the classic arcade game, Kangaroo. Game Overview: Kangaroo is a 1982 arcade game by Sun Electronics (distributed in North America by Atari) where the player controls a mother kangaroo, Mama Roo, to rescue her joey from mischievous monkeys. Gameplay: Mama Roo must navigate platforms and ladders, punching monkeys and collecting fruit while avoiding their attacks. Manual Purpose: The manual provides instructions for operating, maintaining, and servicing the Kangaroo arcade machine, including illustrated parts lists and schematics. Legacy: Kangaroo was later ported to various home consoles, including the Atari 2600 and 5200, and has been released digitally as part of Hamster Corporation's Arcade Archives series. For more information please call or message me. Located in Spencer Tennessee. [hidden information]

ads.author: Scott Nielsen
ads.title: Vintage Midway's Space Invaders Parts Catalog" from October 1978, for Game No. 739. All schematics i
ads.description: Vintage Midway's Space Invaders Parts Catalog" from October 1978, for Game No. 739. All schematics included. About Space Invaders: Space Invaders is a classic fixed shooter arcade game released in 1978 by Taito in Japan and licensed to Midway Manufacturing for distribution in North America. Gameplay: Players control a laser cannon at the bottom of the screen, moving horizontally to shoot down waves of descending aliens and earn points. Cultural Impact: The game was revolutionary for its time, influencing countless other games and shaping popular culture, leading to a shortage of 100-yen coins in Japan due to its immense popularity. Parts Catalog: The catalog shown in the image would contain details and diagrams of the various components used in the Midway Space Invaders arcade machine, assisting with repairs and maintenance. For more information please call or message me. Located in Spencer Tennessee. [hidden information]

ads.author: Scott Nielsen
ads.title: Vintage owner's manual for the Golden Axe arcade game.
ads.description: Vintage owner's manual for the Golden Axe arcade game. If you are interested in purchasing this or similar items, here's some information: Golden Axe arcade game manuals: are available for sale online, primarily on platforms like eBay, where you can find original installation/repair manuals. Full-size Golden Axe arcade machines: are also available for purchase, both pre-owned and new, from various sellers including those specializing in arcade machines. For more information please call or message me. Located in Spencer Tennessee. [hidden information]

ads.author: Scott Nielsen
ads.title: Vintage Universal Kit Installation Instructions manual for the Atari Badlands arcade game
ads.description: Vintage Universal Kit Installation Instructions manual for the Atari Badlands arcade game. This manual is for installing a kit that converts an existing arcade machine into the Atari Badlands game. The Atari Badlands game is a post-apocalyptic racing game with guns, similar to Atari's earlier Super Sprint and Championship Sprint games. The manual includes instructions for installation, self-test procedures, maintenance and troubleshooting, and illustrated parts lists. For more information please call or message me. Located in Spencer Tennessee. [hidden information]

ads.author: John Phillips
ads.title: Vintage Arcade Machine – Working – Original 1980 Conversion Cabinet
ads.description: Up for sale is a vintage Ms. Pac-Man arcade machine in good working condition. This is an original early-80s unit, not a modern reproduction. This machine started life as a cabinet and was professionally converted to Ms. Pac-Man using a period-correct conversion kit (common practice in arcades at the time). The game runs on an original Pac-Man board with the Ms. Pac-Man daughterboard installed. Details: Fully playable Ms. Pac-Man Original CRT monitor (picture looks good for its age) Classic joystick + buttons Cabinet is solid with normal wear from age Side art present (shows some wear, see photos) Original wiring and internal components largely intact Includes original-style operator settings card inside Condition: Works and plays Some cosmetic wear consistent with age (scratches, fading, etc.) Not fully restored – great candidate for restoration or to enjoy as-is Notes for collectors: This is a Pac-Man to Ms. Pac-Man conversion, not a factory-dedicated Ms. Pac-Man cabinet. These were widely used in arcades in the early 1980s and are part of authentic arcade history. Pickup only – located in New Hope AL. Bring help, this is heavy. Message me with any questions or if you want additional photos or video of it running.

ads.author: Gaspar Ferreiro
ads.title: Dedicated Nintendo Vs Arcade Machine with many extras
ads.description: This is a dedicated nintendo vs game system with many extras. For starters, its the dedicated machine, and not a conversion. Second, inside there is a premium switcher board that allows you to switch between 4 boards (and also both sides of a vs board). There is also an adapter board to connect a zapper light gun, and finally it comes with 4 boards. 1-a nintendo vs board with a multi kit that plays 8 games 2-a nintendo vs board that has Mach Rider and DR Mario 3-an atari tetris board (jamma) 4-a Mario Bros boards.

ads.author: David Bolton
ads.title: Pinball machine Hokus Pokus 1975 owned since 1979
ads.description: Edit March 20 We moved into new place and something happened along the way where it is no longer resetting. When you hit the reset button it makes a noise like it's going from one to two player but nothing resets. I know it's something minor. I have lowered the price considerably. Had every switch , blade and contact filed and cleaned and machine shopped out in 2021 by Terri Irwin , a long time respected pinball mechanic in Ocala. Has a few lights out. Only issue is sometimes the score reel does not completely set over and you have to hit the button again to restart it. One of those Ghost in the machine kind of things that she just couldn't figure out . I have owned this game since 1979 when I received it as a kid. You might have seen this up for sale in the past and then the listing disappeared. This was a period of time when I needed money for something so I put it up for sale but then something else came through and I took it down because I didn't really want to sell it. At this point in life it is mostly just nostalgia. I just don't play it anymore. It is very fun game I am just bored with it after 45 years and want something different. Very open to trades for a newer pinball machine plus cash on my end. Thanks for looking! Nine zero four four four four one four three nine.. Dave

ads.author: Michael Sedovic
ads.title: Silent Scope 2 Arcade Machine – Fully Working, FRAM Upgrade (No Suicide Chip)
ads.description: Silent Scope 2: Dark Silhouette arcade cabinet in excellent working condition. Everything works exactly as it should. The screen is bright, the scope is accurate, and controls are responsive. Major upgrade, no more failure risk. This machine has been upgraded with FRAM (FM18W08-PG), eliminating the original Timekeeper RAM / “suicide chip” issue that causes many of these to fail over time. This means no battery dependency, no data loss, and long-term reliability. Features: Fully tested and 100% working Original dedicated cabinet (not a conversion) Scope tracks accurately Strong picture and sound Clean and well-maintained This is a plug-and-play arcade classic, ideal for a home game room or collector. Asking $1,400 OBO Located in Buford Pickup only – I can help load These are getting harder to find in fully working condition with this upgrade. Serious inquiries only

Send me a message with what you have and maybe we can work out a deal. Pickup in Hammond.

ads.author: Lee Barnett
ads.title: Adams family arcade machine missing parts
ads.description: This came in a bundle deal looking to recover cost only missing 2 boards and play field spinner by the looks of it but may be missing more . Never seen one of these before so just going by what I see Pick up Thomas cove Dr groveland FL. We will assist in shipping. You can send your own shipping company in or we work with Woody's haul and ace in shipping machines. Cabinet is in great shape just missing parts sold as is. If post is up it's available

ads.author: Shane Starr
ads.title: Daytona USA - Working Twincab
ads.description: Fully working Daytona Twin Cab; played great. Player 2 CRT tinted slightly green and could use adjustment/repairs but head to head racing works great. Played great in our arcade, but ready to retire to someone else's arcade or for home usage. Card readers will be removed at purchase. Can be set to free play or set up with new readers / coin system.

ads.author: Shane Starr
ads.title: Hang On Project Cab
ads.description: Non working project cab. Cabinet is in great shape but someone stuck an LCD inside with a VGA connection. Includes the board but gets artifacting on power up / display. Board could be repaired.

ads.author: Cory Anderson
ads.title: Carnival Arcade Game
ads.description: I have Carnival arcade game made by Gremlin/Sega in 1980 . Game plays great. I will not answer questions in the comments section. PM me if you interested. Asking $900.00 Firm. Located in Jacksonville, Florida. Shipping anywhere in the country will cost between $500.00-$750.00 through Beltman Logistics / STI.

ads.author: Trussville Woodworks
ads.title: ARCADE GAME
ads.description: Dual seat SEGA GT racer. Old style arcade. Collector's item. Needs TLC but works great. One wheel needs shimmed to use.

ads.author: Scott Knighton
ads.title: Williams MISS-O Pinball Machine
ads.description: Vintage Williams MISS-O pinball machine. Super cool retro pool-themed artwork on the backglass and playfield. Lights are on, ready to play! Has coin slots for nickels and dimes, plus flipper buttons on both sides. Great piece for a game room.

ads.author: Darcy Ferrigno
ads.title: Galaga arcade game by Midway
ads.description: For sale is this original 1981 Galaga Arcade game. I have personally owed this machine for over 30 years. It has been in my personal collection for this time (not Arcade Used). This machine is fully functional and has new locks and keys on coin box and back access panel. Please note this is an original Galaga Arcade Game from 1981. It may have some cabinet scratches and scuff marks etc. (Please see pictures). Game Dimensions: H: 68′′, W: 25′′, D: 27′′ Weight approx. 190 lbs Local pickup or seller will assist if buyer provides, arranges, and pays for shipping company. Price : $1500 OBO

ads.author: Kevin Coughlin
ads.title: Gottlieb Devils Dare Pinball Machine Project
ads.description: Cabinet and populated playfield, no backbox.. in very good condition.. all wiring and mechs intact..

ads.author: Robert Leonard
ads.title: Arcade Machines
ads.description: Multiple arcade machines for sale. Joust, very clean - $1500 Multi-Williams, good shape - $1200 Gauntlet Legends, monitor may need work - $700 Virtua Fighter - $800 Red Neo Geo 4-Slot - $800 STUN Runner - $700 60-in-1 Cocktail, needs new top glass - $500 Ms. Pacman, works, cab in rough shape - $500 Soul Calibur conversion cab, needs work - $300 Pictures here: https://postimg.cc/user/doubletap
